name: West Dean
overview: West Dean is a small village and civil parish on the River Dun in south-east Wiltshire, straddling the Wiltshire/Hampshire border about 7 miles east of Salisbury and a similar distance north-west of Romsey. It has Saxon and Domesday origins (recorded as Deone in 880 and Duene in 1086) and a Roman villa site on the county boundary. Unusually for a village of around 110 households, it has its own station - Dean, on the Wessex Main Line - whose platforms straddle the Wiltshire/Hampshire boundary, with hourly trains towards Salisbury, Romsey and Southampton.
character: A quiet, scattered border village strung along the River Dun and the railway, ringed by farmland and the large Bentley Wood, with a distinct away-from-it-all feel.
amenities: No shop, Post Office or pub remaining in the village
Retains St Mary's Church, the village hall and Dean railway station
Nearest pub and primary school in neighbouring West Tytherley
Main shopping in Salisbury or Romsey

commutes.notes: Hourly South Western Railway service from Dean station in the village

prices.summary: A small village of roughly 110 households, mostly cottages and houses, where the in-village railway station is a notable draw; sales are relatively infrequent. Use the SP5 sold-price record for context.

thingsToDo: Walking and wildlife in Bentley Wood (notable for butterflies)
Borbach Chantry, a Grade I-listed 1333 chapel in the care of the Churches Conservation Trust
Riverside walks along the River Dun and over Dean Hill
The Clarendon Way long-distance path nearby
placesToEat: The Black Horse, West Tytherley (~2 miles)
pros: Rare in-village railway station with direct Salisbury and Southampton trains
Deeply rural border setting with Bentley Wood on the doorstep
Historic church and chantry; quiet, low-traffic lanes
cons: No shop, pub or primary school within the village
Car needed for most day-to-day amenities
Small housing stock; services split across two counties
whoItSuits: Rail commuters and lovers of quiet countryside who want a village with its own station and are happy to travel for shops and schooling.
